svg.title: Spreadsheet-Native Sales Sheets

g.text: Customer-priced inventory browse stays left, while preview, save state, output, history, and Sales Order or Live Shopping handoffs stay visibly explicit on the right.
Legacy route: /sales-sheets · Live Shopping bridge preserved

svg.text: The builder keeps client-priced inventory, preview, dirty-state cues, saved views, history, output controls, and conversion paths visible without collapsing into a modal-heavy sales prep flow.

g.text: The left side stays dominant at review time so the operator can browse client-priced inventory, apply saved views, and add items without losing selection context.
Saved views, filters, and column visibility shape this browser; they do not replace the main inventory review surface.
View profiles save filter/sort state, not frozen item lists
Sticky preview keeps selected items, totals, dirty-state gating, share/export/print outputs, and convert/live actions visible while the inventory browser remains the main curation surface.
Preview remains a secondary companion. It is sticky because save, share, and convert trust cues belong next to selected items.
Dirty edits block share / convert / live
These actions stay explicit. Sales Sheets owns save state, customer-facing output, and live launch; Sales Order owns final composition and transaction workflow after handoff.
4 selected items · default client view active · 2 dirty cells
Golden State · Save first, then share, convert, or go live · selected items persist into Sales Order or Quote
Sales Sheets stays a review-and-output surface; Sales Order owns final transaction workflow and Live Shopping stays a sibling surface.
